gaps.snippet: # [doc = " What a [`LexItem`] is: a real token, or a `//` line comment."] # [derive (Debug , Clone , PartialEq)] pub (crate) enum LexKind { # [doc = " A lexed token (keywords, identifiers, literals, operators, delimiters)."] Token (Tok) , # [doc = " A `//` line comment (runs to end-of-line)."] Comment , }
gaps.reason: enum `LexKind` derive(Clone) is a satisfied no-op under Mycelium's value semantics (ADR-003 — every value already copies structurally; DN-128 §6.1) — not emitted as an impl, not a gap

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " One lexical item resolved to a character span on a single source line. Columns are **1-based**"] # [doc = " (matching [`mycelium_l1::token::Pos`]); LSP consumers subtract one to reach 0-based positions."] # [derive (Debug , Clone , PartialEq)] pub (crate) struct LexItem { # [doc = " 1-based source line of the item's first character."] pub line : u32 , # [doc = " 1-based character column of the item's first character."] pub col : u32 , # [doc = " Length in Unicode scalar values (characters). Exact for ASCII; see module note for non-ASCII."] pub len : u32 , # [doc = " The token or comment this span covers."] pub kind : LexKind , }
gaps.reason: struct `LexItem` uses named fields and references a type not resolvable in-file — emitting it positionally would introduce an unresolved reference that poisons the file's `myc check`; left gapped under the M-1006 resolvability gate (VR-5/G2)

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Resolve every token and comment in `src` to a character span (see module note for the"] # [doc = " boundary-recovery method)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " **Never-silent (G2):** on a lexer error this returns an **empty** list rather than fabricating"] # [doc = " spans — the parse error itself is reported on the diagnostics channel ([`crate::sync`]); this"] # [doc = " layer simply has nothing it can honestly highlight. The `Tok::Eof` sentinel is excluded (it has"] # [doc = " no source text)."] pub (crate) fn lex_items (src : & str) -> Vec < LexItem > { if ! src . is_ascii () { return Vec :: new () ; } let (toks , comments) = match lex_with_comments (src) { Ok (pair) => pair , Err (_) => return Vec :: new () , } ; let line_chars : Vec < Vec < char > > = src . split ('\n') . map (| l | l . strip_suffix ('\r') . unwrap_or (l) . chars () . collect ()) . collect () ; let line_len = | line : u32 | -> u32 { line_chars . get (line . saturating_sub (1) as usize) . map_or (0 , | c | c . len () as u32) } ; # [derive (Clone)] struct Raw { line : u32 , col : u32 , kind : LexKind , } let mut raws : Vec < Raw > = Vec :: with_capacity (toks . len () + comments . len ()) ; for s in toks { if s . tok == Tok :: Eof { continue ; } raws . push (Raw { line : s . pos . line , col : s . pos . col , kind : LexKind :: Token (s . tok) , }) ; } for c in comments { raws . push (Raw { line : c . line , col : c . col , kind : LexKind :: Comment , }) ; } raws . sort_by_key (| a | (a . line , a . col)) ; let mut out = Vec :: with_capacity (raws . len ()) ; for i in 0 .. raws . len () { let r = & raws [i] ; let len = match & r . kind { LexKind :: Comment => line_len (r . line) . saturating_sub (r . col . saturating_sub (1)) , LexKind :: Token (_) => { let raw_end_col = match raws . get (i + 1) { Some (next) if next . line == r . line => next . col , _ => line_len (r . line) + 1 , } ; token_len (& line_chars , r . line , r . col , raw_end_col) } } ; if len == 0 { continue ; } out . push (LexItem { line : r . line , col : r . col , len , kind : r . kind . clone () , }) ; } out }
gaps.reason: function body has a semicolon-terminated (value-discarding) statement expression before the tail — a `let`-chain body maps only simple `let x = e;` bindings plus a single trailing expression

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " The trimmed character length of a token occupying `[col, raw_end_col)` (1-based, exclusive end)"] # [doc = " on `line` of `line_chars`: the raw span minus any trailing whitespace (the trivia separating it"] # [doc = " from the next boundary)."] fn token_len (line_chars : & [Vec < char >] , line : u32 , col : u32 , raw_end_col : u32) -> u32 { let Some (chars) = line_chars . get (line . saturating_sub (1) as usize) else { return 0 ; } ; let start = col . saturating_sub (1) as usize ; let raw_end = (raw_end_col . saturating_sub (1) as usize) . min (chars . len ()) ; if raw_end <= start { return 0 ; } let mut end = raw_end ; while end > start && chars [end - 1] . is_whitespace () { end -= 1 ; } (end - start) as u32 }
gaps.reason: `let` binding uses an unsupported pattern (only simple `let x = e;` is supported)

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " The [`LexItem`] whose span covers the 0-based LSP position `(line0, char0)`, if any. The span is"] # [doc = " the half-open character interval `[col-1, col-1+len)` on `line-1`. Returns `None` when the"] # [doc = " position falls on whitespace, a delimiter gap, or outside the document — **never-silent**: an"] # [doc = " absent target is an explicit `None`, never a fabricated span (G2)."] pub (crate) fn item_at (items : & [LexItem] , line0 : u32 , char0 : u32) -> Option < & LexItem > { let line = line0 . checked_add (1) ? ; items . iter () . find (| it | { it . line == line && char0 >= it . col . saturating_sub (1) && char0 < it . col . saturating_sub (1) + it . len }) }

" ; assert ! (lex_items (non_ascii) . is_empty () , "non-ASCII source must yield no spans (UTF-16 stopgap), never mis-offset ranges") ; } # [test] fn token_lengths_are_exact_for_adjacent_and_spaced_tokens () { let items = item_kinds ("nodule x
